title: About Me!
text:
Hi there! I’m Laura (pronounced ““). I am an Associate Professor of Statistics in the Department of Mathematics, Statistics, and Computer Science at Lawrence University, Appleton, Wisconsin. I am originally from Kolkata, India. I completed my Bachelors in Statistics from St. Xavier’s College, Kolkata, and my Masters from IIT, Kanpur. I earned my Ph.D. in Statistics from Iowa State University.
I enjoy teaching and relish being in the company of students. Please visit my teaching and research pages to learn more about my academic activities.
Outside the academic world, I enjoy running, biking, and going for hikes. My other interests include watching soccer as well as fiddling with my guitar.

title: Laura Henze!
text:
Hi there! I’m Laura (pronounced “LOR-uh”). I am an Undergraduate Student at Lawrence University in the Biochemistry, Data Science, and Anthropology departments. I’m a Wisconsin native and have kept my roots here ever since I was born. Prior to my time at Lawrence, I graduated near the top of my class from my alma mater, Plymouth High School where I gained my love for science. In the future, I plan to attend a Forensic Science graduate institute in order to specialize in Forensic Toxicology.
Through my explorations of research at Lawrence, I came to discover that data processing was something that I didn’t know about, but seemed like a handy skill to have. With this, I discovered my enjoyment of data science and producing informative figures for my technical lab reports.
In addition to my academics, I compete on the Lawrence Cross Country and Track and Field teams in my extra time. Running has continued to be one of my passions since I was young and I’m very greatful to represent my team here. In addition to running and my studies, I also enjoy singing, watching movies, playing videogames, and practicing my flute (when I have the time).
